test_plan.strategy: Prove fidelity of the v0.3.0 redesign to its FRs on the existing Hub stack. Unit tests for pure derivations (health rank, name translation, tile mapping); integration tests for the new/extended endpoints (artifact content, QA executions, detail reads) exercised against a temp ~/.aitri-hub and fixture project dirs; e2e (Playwright) for the two critical flows (Dev triage, QA execution). Security and regression concerns are tested as negative/edge TCs traced to the FR they protect, cross-referenced to the NFR via frs[]. All fixtures use os.tmpdir()/relative paths, never absolute.
test_plan.coverage_goal: 80% line + every MUST FR at h/e/f

test_cases.title: Monitor sizes+orders CRITICAL card largest and first
test_cases.then: card 'a' has gridColumnSpan=2 and index 0; order is [a, b or c, ..., d, e] (critical<at_risk<healthy)
test_cases.expected_result: 'a' spans 2 columns and is first; healthy cards last

test_cases.title: Card shrinks+moves to end when health drops CRITICAL→NOMINAL
test_cases.given: project 'a' was critical (span=2, index 0); next snapshot sets a.health=healthy
test_cases.then: card 'a' gridColumnSpan=1 and appears after all critical+at_risk cards (last group)
test_cases.expected_result: 'a' is compact and ordered into the NOMINAL group

test_cases.title: CRITICAL filter with zero critical projects yields empty grid + count 0
test_cases.then: result=[] and filterBarCount('CRITICAL')===0; grid renders the '0 matching' state, not a broken layout
steps: filter a no-critical set by CRITICAL
test_cases.expected_result: empty result, count 0, explicit empty grid state

test_cases.title: Card renders 6 tiles and pipeline filled to approved count
test_cases.then: model.tiles.length===6 (Tests,Drift,Verify,Pending,Signals,Rejections) and pipeline segments filled===3 of 5
steps: build card model for a 3-approved project

test_cases.title: Top-issue line shows highest-severity issue text
test_cases.then: model.topIssue==='verify failed' (critical outranks warn)
steps: pick top issue from mixed-severity list
test_cases.expected_result: the critical issue text is selected

test_cases.title: Tile shows N/A (not 0) when datum absent
test_cases.then: model.tiles.find(t=>t.key==='Tests').value==='N/A' and .value!=='0'
steps: build card for a project with no verify run
test_cases.expected_result: Tests/Verify tiles read 'N/A', never a misleading 0

test_cases.title: Sidebar section badge equals section issue count
test_cases.given: project health has 2 issues in dimension 'tests'
test_cases.then: sidebar.nav.find(n=>n.section==='health').badge===2

test_cases.title: Browser back returns to Monitor preserving the prior filter
test_cases.given: Monitor filter='CRITICAL'; user opened project 'a' detail (history.pushState)
test_cases.then: view==='monitor' and activeFilter==='CRITICAL' and no full reload (document not re-fetched)
test_cases.expected_result: monitor view with CRITICAL filter intact

test_cases.title: Detail for a removed project id shows not-found panel, no crash
test_cases.given: selectedProjectId='ghost' not present in current dashboard.json
test_cases.then: a 'Project not found' panel with a 'Back to Monitor' action renders; no thrown error
steps: open detail for an absent id

test_cases.title: Overview gauge reflects testSummary pass ratio
test_cases.then: gauge.ratio===1.0 and telemetry shows 27/0/0/27 + last-run age

test_cases.title: Phase labels are readable, not technical filenames
test_cases.then: overview.phases[0].label matches /requirements|PRD/i and no label contains '01_REQUIREMENTS.json'

test_cases.title: No verify run shows 'no run yet', not a zeroed gauge
test_cases.then: telemetry.state==='no-run-yet' and gauge is not rendered as 0/0
steps: build overview with no verify run

test_cases.title: Health renders 5 dimensions; failing test dim is CRITICAL/WARN, version names both
test_cases.given: project health.issues include a failing test and a version mismatch project 2.0.1 vs CLI 2.0.2
test_cases.then: panels.length===5 in order [Pipeline,Tests,Code,Artifacts,Version]; Tests.badge in {WARN,CRITICAL}; Version.text contains '2.0.1' and '2.0.2'
test_cases.expected_result: 5 ordered panels; version names both versions

test_cases.title: Issue with known remediation shows the remediation command
test_cases.given: a Version-dimension issue with remediation 'aitri adopt --upgrade'
test_cases.then: Version panel issue.remediation==='aitri adopt --upgrade'
test_cases.expected_result: remediation command shown with the issue

test_cases.title: Dimension with no issues shows 'all checks passing', not empty
test_cases.given: project health has zero issues in dimension 'code'
test_cases.then: Code panel badge==='OK' and body text==='All checks passing'; panel is not blank

test_cases.title: Detail endpoint groups artifacts by phase with rolled-up folder glyph
preconditions: temp project dir with spec/ artifacts of mixed status
test_cases.given: project spec has phase_1 artifacts all status=approved, phase_2 has one status=rejected
test_cases.then: response artifacts.phase_1.glyph==='approved'(✓) and phase_2.glyph==='rejected'(✕)
test_cases.expected_result: folder glyph = worst child status per phase

test_cases.title: Re-selecting an open file toggles the reader closed
test_cases.then: state.selected===null (reader closed) and no file marked active

test_cases.title: Explorer with no selection shows 'select a file to read'
test_cases.then: reader shows empty state text 'select a file to read' and no content pane

test_cases.title: Markdown artifact renders formatted with inline PNG, no raw syntax
preconditions: temp project with spec/doc.md containing a heading, a table, and ![w](./a.png)
test_cases.given: GET target project p1, path='spec/doc.md' with a relative PNG that exists
test_cases.then:
output DOM has

, , and resolved; no literal '#' or '|' markdown tokens visible

test_cases.title: Unresolved image degrades to alt-text placeholder, doc still renders
test_cases.given: artifact markdown references a non-existent image with alt='diagram'
test_cases.then: an alt-text placeholder reading 'diagram' renders, the rest of the document renders, and no exception is thrown
steps: render markdown with a missing image

test_cases.title: Artifact path traversal is rejected with 403
test_cases.then: response status 403 and body { error, code:'confinement' }; /etc/passwd contents are NOT returned

test_cases.title: Sessions render newest-first with inline rejection feedback
test_cases.then: timeline[0].event==='rejected' (newest first) and timeline[0].feedback==='fix tokens'

test_cases.title: Last-session context shows files-touched chips and agent
test_cases.then: context.agent==='claude' and context.files.length===2 rendered as chips

test_cases.title: No events shows explicit empty state
test_cases.then: sessions.state==='empty' with explanatory text; no blank panel

test_cases.title: Alerts merge health issues + external signals with remediation command
test_cases.then: alerts.length===3 and the signal card.command==='npm audit fix'

test_cases.title: Alerts count equals sidebar badge (issues + signals)
test_cases.then: alerts.length===3 and sidebar.nav.find(n=>n.section==='alerts').badge===3

test_cases.title: Zero alerts shows all-clear confirmation, not a blank panel
test_cases.then: alerts.state==='all-clear' with confirmation visual; panel is not empty

test_cases.title: Technical name maps to product name as primary label
test_cases.then: returns 'PRD — Product Requirements'; the row's secondary text still holds '01_REQUIREMENTS.json'
test_cases.expected_result: PRD label primary, technical name secondary

test_cases.title: Feature artifact product name is prefixed with feature name
test_cases.then: returns a label starting with 'redesign' and containing 'PRD'

test_cases.title: Unmapped name falls back to raw filename, no crash/blank
test_cases.then: returns 'NOTES.txt' (raw), does not throw, and is not empty string

test_cases.title: Test cases list groups by phase/feature; manual filter narrows set
test_cases.then: listed cases.length===2 and the manual-count===2

test_cases.title: Manual case status edit persists and reflects on reload
test_cases.then: TC-M1 override status==='passed' persists under ~/.aitri-hub and survives reload

test_cases.title: Editing an automated case status is blocked (409)
test_cases.then: response status 409 { error, code:'automated_case' } and TC-A1 status is unchanged

test_cases.title: Manual execution persists bound to resultsBinding+runStamp with evidence
test_cases.given: POST /api/project/p1/testcases/TC-M1/executions { result:'passed', notes:'ok', environment:'local', evidence:{filename:'s.png', base64:<valid png>} }
test_cases.then: status 201; executions.json for p1 has a record with binding={resultsBinding:'bound',runStamp:'rs-1'}, evidenceRef set, and an evidence file exists under ~/.aitri-hub/qa/p1/evidence/
test_cases.expected_result: 201, record + evidence persisted, bound to current run

test_cases.title: Second execution appends; history additive and survives restart
test_cases.given: a second POST execution for TC-M1 { result:'failed' }
test_cases.when: the request is handled, then the store is re-read fresh (simulating restart)
test_cases.then: executions for TC-M1 length===2 in chronological order; the first record is unchanged (no overwrite)

test_cases.title: Execution with no result is rejected 400; nothing persisted
test_cases.then: status 400 { error, code:'result_required' } and executions.json for TC-M1 remains empty (no partial write)

test_cases.title: Oversized/disallowed evidence rejected before persistence
test_cases.given: POST execution with evidence {filename:'e.exe', base64:<6MB non-image>}
test_cases.then: status 415 or 413 { code in ('bad_evidence_type','evidence_too_large') } and no evidence file is written and no execution appended

test_cases.title: Bugs list from BUGS.json; severity filter narrows set
test_cases.then: listed bugs.length===1 with severity==='high'

test_cases.title: Bug detail shows repro, evidence and status history
test_cases.given: bug BUG-1 = {description:'crash on load', repro:'steps', evidence:'shot.png', statusHistory:[open,fixed]}
test_cases.then: detail renders ALL of: full description 'crash on load', repro steps, the evidence 'shot.png', and a 2-entry status history (per AC-022-2 — none of the four dropped)
test_cases.expected_result: description + repro + evidence + history all shown

test_cases.title: Malformed BUGS.json surfaces parseError, never silent 0
test_cases.then: bugs.parseError===true and the UI shows a WARN banner 'Bug file could not be read' — NOT '0 bugs'

test_cases.title: Project-summary report renders on-demand from current snapshot
test_cases.then: report body includes coverage, bugsBySeverity, and case pass/fail/pending counts computed from the current snapshot

test_cases.title: Report print view drops navigation chrome
test_cases.given: the project report is open in the browser
test_cases.then: the sidebar/topbar nav chrome has display:none in print and only the report body is visible

test_cases.title: Report scope with no data shows empty-report state
test_cases.then: response 200 with report.state==='empty' (explicit empty-report), not a broken/blank layout or 500

test_cases.title: Refresh timer re-sorts grid from new snapshot without reload
test_cases.given: poller active; snapshot v1 order [a(healthy),b(critical)]; snapshot v2 makes a critical
test_cases.when: the refresh timer fires and fetches v2
test_cases.then: grid re-sorts to [a,b] with a first (now critical) and window.location is unchanged (no reload)
steps: tick timer with a changed snapshot

test_cases.title: No manual refresh control exists in the UI
test_cases.when: query the DOM for any element with role/label 'refresh'
test_cases.then: zero refresh controls are found (refresh is timer-only in v1)

test_cases.title: Stale/failed snapshot read keeps last data + shows stale indicator
test_cases.given: last good snapshot rendered; next fetch of /data/dashboard.json rejects (network error)
test_cases.when: the refresh handler processes the failure
test_cases.then: the previously-rendered projects remain visible and a 'snapshot stale' indicator is shown; the grid is not blanked

test_cases.title: Loopback peer is allowed on a QA write
test_cases.given: a request from peer 127.0.0.1 POSTs a valid execution
test_cases.when: the loopback guard evaluates the peer
test_cases.then: the guard passes and the handler returns 201 (not 403)

test_cases.title: Non-loopback peer is rejected 403 on a QA write
test_cases.given: a request whose remoteAddress is 192.168.1.50 POSTs to /api/project/p1/testcases/TC-M1/executions
test_cases.when: the loopback guard evaluates the peer
test_cases.then: response status 403 and no execution is written

test_cases.title: Symlink escaping the allow-root is rejected
test_cases.given: artifact path='spec/link/passwd' where spec/link symlinks outside the project
test_cases.then: realpath containment fails and response is 403; no external file content is returned

test_cases.title: dashboard.json field parity vs golden baseline
preconditions: fixture projects + a golden dashboard.json baseline
test_cases.given: the collector runs over the fixture set post-redesign
test_cases.when: the emitted dashboard.json is diffed against the golden baseline
test_cases.then: the set of top-level project fields (aitriState, gitMeta, testSummary, health, specArtifacts, externalSignals) matches the baseline with zero removed keys

test_cases.title: New detail data does not mutate the frozen snapshot
test_cases.given: a QA execution is recorded and the artifact endpoint is called
test_cases.then: dashboard.json is byte-identical to before the QA/artifact calls (QA state lives only under ~/.aitri-hub/qa)

test_cases.title: Malformed .aitri yields unreadable status, no crash
preconditions: a project with a corrupt .aitri file
test_cases.when: the collector runs and the card renders
test_cases.then: p2 record has aitriState===null and status 'unreadable'; the collector completes without throwing and other projects still collect
test_cases.expected_result: unreadable card, no crash, others unaffected

test_cases.title: aitri-hub monitor collection produces the same project set+status post-redesign
preconditions: fixture set of 3 projects + golden baseline
test_cases.given: the collector runs over the 3-project fixture after the redesign
test_cases.when: the emitted project records are compared to the golden baseline
test_cases.then: the same 3 project ids appear with identical derived status values (healthy/warning/error) as the baseline
steps: run collector, compare status per project
test_cases.expected_result: same projects, same statuses as baseline

test_cases.title: Remote/cached project still collects the same fields as before
preconditions: one remote-type project with a warm cache
test_cases.given: a remote project whose cache exists and is unchanged
test_cases.then: the remote project's aitriState, gitMeta and testSummary match the pre-redesign baseline for the same cache

test_cases.title: One project throwing mid-collection does not abort the whole monitor run
preconditions: 3 projects, one with an unreadable git dir
test_cases.given: project p2's git metadata read throws
test_cases.then: p2 collects with null git fields and no throw; p1 and p3 still produce complete records (run not aborted)

test_cases.title: Valid .aitri + 04_TEST_RESULTS extract the same fields as baseline
test_cases.then: aitriState.approvedPhases, aitriState.currentPhase, testSummary and rejections match the pre-redesign baseline exactly
steps: collect a valid project, diff fields
test_cases.expected_result: ingestion parity for a valid project

test_cases.title: Unknown extra key in .aitri is ignored (forward-compatible), fields still extracted
preconditions: project whose .aitri has an unrecognized extra top-level key
test_cases.given: a .aitri file with a valid schema plus an unknown key 'futureFlag'
test_cases.then: the known fields extract normally and 'futureFlag' is ignored without error
steps: collect a .aitri with an extra key

test_cases.title: JSON artifact renders as a structured projection, not a raw text dump
test_cases.then: the reader shows labeled keys (project_name, functional_requirements) as a structured tree with FR-001 as a row; the DOM does NOT contain the raw string '{"project_name"' as a single text node
test_cases.expected_result: structured projection, not raw JSON text

test_cases.title: Overview six metric tiles display the project's actual values
test_cases.given: project lastSession.at='2026-07-17', lastSession.agent='claude', gitMeta.branch='main', testSummary passed=27, aitriState.commitsPending=3, appVersion='0.2.2'
test_cases.then: tiles show last_session='2026-07-17', agent='claude', branch='main', verify='27 passed', pending_commits=3, version='0.2.2' — each equal to the source datum, none blank or hardcoded
steps: assert each of the 6 tile values against the fixture
test_cases.expected_result: all six tiles show actual values

test_cases.title: Sidebar quick-stats equal the Monitor card values (cross-view parity)
test_cases.given: project with activeIssues=4, rejections=1, drift=2, tests='27/27'
test_cases.when: buildSidebar(project) and buildCardModel(project) run from the same record
test_cases.then: sidebar.quickStats {issues:4, rejections:1, drift:2, tests:'27/27'} deep-equals the card's corresponding values
steps: compare sidebar quick-stats to card values
test_cases.expected_result: quick-stats match card (no drift between views)

test_cases.title: Absolute path is rejected 403
test_cases.then: response status 403 { code:'confinement' } and no /etc/passwd content is returned

test_cases.title: URL-encoded traversal (%2e%2e) is rejected after decode
test_cases.then: the handler decodes then applies confinement and returns 403; a raw-string '..' filter that skips the decoded value would leak — this asserts the decode boundary is checked

test_cases.title: Magic-byte spoof (png name+MIME, non-PNG bytes) is rejected
test_cases.given: POST execution evidence {filename:'s.png', mime:'image/png', base64:<bytes whose leading magic is 'MZ' (a PE exe), not the PNG signature 89 50 4E 47>}
test_cases.then: status 415 { code:'bad_evidence_type' } (server validates magic bytes, not the declared MIME); no evidence file written, no execution appended

test_cases.title: Client filename with path separators is ignored; stored name is server-generated
test_cases.when: the request is handled and succeeds
test_cases.then: the stored evidence file lives at ~/.aitri-hub/qa/p1/evidence/<uuid>.png (server-generated name); NO file is created outside that dir and the client filename is not used in the path
steps: post evidence with a malicious filename
test_cases.expected_result: server-generated path, no escape via filename

test_cases.title: SVG evidence with embedded script is sanitized or served non-inline
test_cases.when: the evidence is stored and later served/rendered
test_cases.then: either the stored/served SVG has the <script> stripped (sanitized), OR it is served with Content-Disposition: attachment + a restrictive CSP; it is NEVER rendered inline with the script intact
steps: upload a scripted SVG, inspect how it is served
test_cases.expected_result: no inline script execution (sanitized or attachment)

test_cases.title: IPv6 loopback (::1) is allowed on a QA write
test_cases.given: a request whose remoteAddress is '::1' POSTs a valid execution
test_cases.when: the loopback guard evaluates the peer
test_cases.then: the guard passes (::1 and ::ffff:127.0.0.1 are treated as loopback) and the handler returns 201, not 403

test_cases.title: E2E Dev triage: Monitor → open CRITICAL → Health → back preserves filter
preconditions: Hub served with a seeded snapshot containing one CRITICAL project
test_cases.given: the Monitor is open at localhost with filter set to CRITICAL
test_cases.when: the user clicks the CRITICAL card, opens the Health section, then presses browser Back
test_cases.then: the detail opened on the correct project, Health showed 5 dimension panels with the failing one CRITICAL, and Back returned to the Monitor still filtered to CRITICAL (no full reload)
test_cases.expected_result: full triage flow works, filter preserved

test_cases.title: E2E QA: select manual case → record execution with evidence → appears in history
preconditions: Hub served; project with a manual test case TC-M1
test_cases.given: the QA Test Cases section is open on project p1
test_cases.when: the user selects TC-M1, records an execution result=passed with a PNG evidence and submits
test_cases.then: a success state shows and the execution appears at the top of TC-M1's history bound to the current run
test_cases.expected_result: execution recorded and visible in history

test_cases.title: E2E QA: submitting execution with no result shows inline error, nothing saved
preconditions: QA execution form open for TC-M1
test_cases.given: the execution form is open with no result selected
test_cases.then: an inline field error 'Select a result before saving' appears and no new execution is added to TC-M1's history
